summary: Iteration 2 — Tested Resend email notifications (approve/reject) + VIP daily-closing PDF. Backend: 37/38 pytest passed (25 regression + 12 new). 1 real bug found via the double-approve test. Frontend VIP /dashboard/vip 'Cierre Diario' card renders correctly, date input prefilled with today (2026-06-14), and PDF downloads work for both today and a custom past date (2025-12-15). PDFs validated: %PDF magic + size 2.7–3.4 KB. No console errors.

critical.issue: DOUBLE-CREDIT BUG: When admin approves an order with delivery_method='accumulate' twice (e.g. via duplicate click or status reapplied), vip_balance_usd is incremented twice. The accumulate $inc block (server.py ~L415-419) has no `order['status'] != new_status` guard, while the email-notification block (L422) correctly does. Reproduced: order amount_to=2765 USD credited twice → +5530 USD instead of +2765. Fix: wrap the $inc inside `if order['status'] != 'approved':`.

minor.issue: PDF for an empty date (e.g. 2020-01-01) renders a placeholder row of em-dashes — acceptable but could show a clearer empty state.

Sender is onboarding@resend.dev (no verified domain). Resend free tier may restrict delivery to the account owner's email; production-ready setup requires verifying notificacionesresiliencebrothe@gmail.com domain or adding it as a verified recipient. Endpoint behaviour is correct (try/except wraps the send, so 200 is returned regardless).

issues: Native browser date picker is used (matches the rest of the app's <Input type="date">) — visually fine in dark theme but inconsistent with shadcn Calendar if that's desired elsewhere. Low priority.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): Add `if order['status'] != 'approved':` guard around the accumulate $inc in PUT /api/admin/orders/{id}/status to prevent double-crediting VIP balance on duplicate approve clicks.
(Optional) Verify a real sender domain in Resend so production emails are delivered to clients (currently using onboarding@resend.dev).
(Optional) Show a friendlier 'Sin órdenes aprobadas hoy' message in the PDF when the day has zero approved orders, instead of em-dashes.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py update_order_status: balance-accumulate is not idempotent (HIGH — see above). The status-change guard exists for email but not for balance.

server.py now ~680 lines — still acceptable but the new endpoints push it close to the 700-line guideline. Consider extracting `/admin/orders/*`, `/vip/*` (closing + withdrawals + redemptions) into routers.
email_service._send correctly wraps resend.Emails.send in try/except and logs failures. Good defensive design.
pdf_service.generate_vip_closing_pdf handles the no-orders case (placeholder row) and uses iso-aware date parsing — robust.

/api/vip/daily-closing correctly uses UTC day boundaries with `updated_at` ISO-string comparison. Note: relies on `updated_at` lexicographic ordering matching chronological — works because the format is ISO-8601 with fixed tz offset. Good.
logger usage at server.py L431 references module-level `logger` defined at L673 — works due to late binding, but cleaner to define logger near top of file before first use.
Frontend VipView uses axios with responseType='blob' and creates an object URL for download — standard pattern, works in tests.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_email_and_closing.py (new — 13 tests covering email-safe status updates + PDF closing)

success_rate.backend: 97% (37/38) — 1 failure is a real production bug, not a test issue

test_credentials: Reused iteration 1 sessions: admin=test_session_admin_X, vip=test_session_vip_X, normal=test_session_normal_X (in MongoDB).
seed_data_creation: No new seed. Reused existing seed and created ~4 VIP transient orders during testing (approved/rejected/completed/pending).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v` to execute both suites. The single failing test `test_double_approve_only_credits_once` documents a real bug — once main agent adds the status-change guard around the accumulate $inc, this test will pass. Frontend VIP closing PDF download works end-to-end via the preview URL.
rca of the issue: Double-credit RCA: in PUT /api/admin/orders/{id}/status, the balance increment runs on every call where new_status=='approved' and delivery_method=='accumulate', without checking the prior status. Email block correctly uses `order['status'] != new_status` guard. Reproduction: create VIP accumulate order → PUT status=approved twice → vip_balance_usd grows by 2*amount_to. Mitigation: wrap the $inc in the same guard used for email (`if order['status'] != 'approved'`).
